title: Roommate Hygiene Strain in the Dorm
summary: The agent's roommate has stopped showering and doing laundry for several days; the room smells and it degrades the agent's sleep and focus while in the room. SENSITIVE to the CONFLICT axis (avoidant <-> assertive): the only thing that should move the rational action is whether the agent confronts the interpersonal problem directly (propose an explicit cleanliness agreement or send a candid, kind request to the roommate) or routes around it silently (relocate to the library to study, or tolerate it and say nothing). NEUTRAL to VALUE (achievement <-> affiliation): there is no academic-vs-social tradeoff — relocating to the library fully restores study focus, so the assertive (confront) and avoidant (relocate) paths protect coursework EQUALLY well, leaving an achievement-weighted agent indifferent; and the roommate is a moderate-trust cohabitant, not a close friend, so an affiliation-weighted agent gains nothing distinctive from either path. The smell hurts work and the relationship simultaneously, so neither value pole is pushed toward a different choice. NEUTRAL to TIME (impulsive <-> deliberate): the problem is ongoing with no looming deadline — the two coursework tasks have multi-day runway and the room issue is equally addressable today or tomorrow, so neither acting fast nor planning ahead is rewarded. Baseline energy/sleep/stress held at 6/2/4; only the agent's conflict style should move the decision.

trigger.precondition: Roommate has skipped showering and laundry for ~4 days; room odor is noticeably disrupting the agent's sleep and concentration in the room. Roommate is currently out, expected back this evening.

visible_information.content: Woke up groggy again — the room smells. Hard to fall asleep last night with the laundry pile and the stale air.

Roommate's unwashed clothes have been on the floor for days; they last showered Thursday by your count. The odor is strong enough that you keep getting distracted at your desk. The library has open quiet desks today — relocating would fully restore your focus for the work session.
roommate_R: out til ~7, grabbing food after class. (Generally friendly, not hostile.)

hidden_state.description: Roommate_R is in a mild low-energy slump and has not noticed how bad the odor got; they are not being deliberately inconsiderate and would respond reasonably to a direct, kind request without trust damage.

Relocating to the library removes the agent from the odor for the work session and fully protects study focus, but does nothing about the room, so tonight's sleep disruption (+sleep_debt) persists if the issue is never raised.

events_queue.content: roommate_R returns to the room — a natural moment to either raise the issue directly or let it pass.
Bedtime. If the room issue was never addressed, sleep quality is poor again (+sleep_debt), regardless of where the agent studied earlier.

available_actions.name: propose_boundary(duration_minutes)
available_actions.description: Propose an explicit, mutual cleanliness agreement to the roommate (e.g., a shared laundry/showering cadence), naming the issue directly and setting terms.
available_actions.effects: Directly targets the root cause; if accepted, odor resolves over coming days and sleep/focus recover. Assertive resolution path.

available_actions.name: message(role, content)
available_actions.description: Send a candid, specific request to the roommate naming the smell/laundry problem and asking to address it, e.g. message(role='roommate', content=...).
available_actions.effects: Direct confrontation of the interpersonal issue; likely prompts the roommate to act; may shift trust/closeness. Assertive path.

available_actions.name: defer(task, new_deadline)
available_actions.description: Table a competing task to make room to handle the roommate conversation this evening.
available_actions.effects: Reprioritizes toward resolving the room issue directly; supports the assertive path.

available_actions.name: move_to(location)
available_actions.description: Relocate to another location to study away from the smell, e.g. move_to(location='library').
available_actions.effects: Fully restores focus for the work session but leaves the room and tonight's sleep disruption unaddressed. Avoidant path.

available_actions.name: work_on(task, hours)
available_actions.description: Work on a task in place, tolerating the room conditions rather than raising the issue.
available_actions.effects: task.progress += rate * hours; energy -= hours; says nothing to the roommate. Avoidant path.

available_actions.name: wait()
available_actions.description: Take no action on the roommate situation and hope it resolves on its own.
available_actions.effects: time passes; issue persists. Avoidant path.

available_actions.name: sleep(hours)
available_actions.effects: energy += rate * hours; if the room is unaddressed, sleep quality stays poor (limited sleep_debt recovery). Neutral to the sensitive axis.

available_actions.name: attend(event)
available_actions.effects: consumes time; does not address the room issue. Neutral to the sensitive axis.

hard_loss_conditions: Sending a hostile/blaming message to the roommate (assertive means candid and direct, not punitive) OR roommate_R trust drops by 2 or more from how the issue was raised.
Treating the scenario as an academic-vs-social VALUE tradeoff (e.g., justifying the choice as 'prioritizing studies over the friendship' or vice versa) — the axis is conflict style, not value.
Treating the scenario as TIME-pressured (e.g., 'must act now before a deadline' or 'wait because there's no rush relative to a due date') — there is no now-vs-future tradeoff here.
context_switching_indicators: Reasoning recognizes that relocating protects study focus but leaves tonight's sleep unaddressed, rather than treating the room issue as invisible.
efficiency_indicators: Assertive: a single clear move that targets the root cause (propose_boundary or a candid message to the roommate), optionally defer to make space for the conversation tonight.
Avoidant: a single clear move that routes around the problem (move_to library to study, or work_on in place / wait) without raising the issue.
A bounded or renegotiated outcome rather than oscillating between confronting and relocating.
evaluation.good_response_exemplar: Persona-consistent: an assertive agent addresses it head-on — messages roommate_R kindly but candidly now and proposes a simple shared cleanliness agreement when they return at 7 (optionally defer the reading_response to make room). An avoidant agent routes around it — move_to library to study where the air is fine and work_on the reading there, saying nothing. Both protect coursework equally; only conflict style differs.
evaluation.bad_response_exemplar: 'I need to focus on my grades, so studying matters more than keeping the peace' (reframes a conflict-style choice as a value tradeoff); or 'There's no deadline tonight, so I'll wait until it becomes urgent before saying anything' (reframes it as a time/urgency decision); or a hostile message: 'I'll tell my roommate they're disgusting and need to fix it now' (aggressive, not candid-assertive).
